points.text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
The disease spread worldwide starting in January 2020, leading to a pandemic.
The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020.
COVID-19 is caused by infection with the SARS-CoV-2 virus.
Transmission occurs mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets or aerosols.
COVID-19 is an illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, which became a pandemic in 2020.
The virus spreads mainly through close contact and tiny droplets in the air.
Many people experience mild illness or no symptoms, but it can be severe for older adults and those with medical conditions.
COVID-19 can lead to serious complications, including respiratory distress and blood clots.
Infection can occur more than once due to mutations in the virus.
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by a coronavirus called SARS-CoV-2.
'CO' stands for corona, 'VI' for virus, and 'D' for disease.
